oneLineSummary: A booking experience concept focused on accommodation browsing, mobile-friendly decision flow, and clearer trip-planning interactions across key screens.

summary.systemDefinition: A frontend-led booking app concept for accommodation discovery and trip planning.
coreWorkflowsFeatures: Mobile-first layout decisions for trip planning
Clearer screen hierarchy for booking-related actions
summary.businessOrOperationalImpact: The concept explores how stronger information hierarchy can reduce friction in travel booking decisions.
summary.engineeringValue: It demonstrates product flow thinking, responsive interface planning, and structured case-study communication.

projectNarrative.problem: Travel booking journeys can become confusing when too much information competes for attention across small screens.
projectNarrative.approach: I explored a more structured accommodation browsing and decision-making flow with clearer screen hierarchy and mobile responsiveness.
projectNarrative.result: The case study shows how frontend clarity and interaction structure can improve a booking experience.

engineeringHighlights: Responsive UI planning for booking flows
UX flow simplification for decision-heavy screens

reflection.tradeoffs: Improving browsing clarity required deciding which information to delay so the user journey stayed focused instead of overloaded.
lessonsLearned: Booking products benefit from clearer priority ordering across screens.
Case studies are stronger when interaction rationale is explicit.
futureImprovements: Prototype validation with task-based user testing

display.libraryFocus: front-end clarity, booking journey structure, and user decision support
